page: Amazon Web Services gr6.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, containing 22 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and network performance is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads, particularly in LLM inference. The instance achieves top-tier performance in prompt processing and text generation for small and medium models, alongside strong prompt processing for large models. General compute benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, place the instance in the average tier. However, single-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is also strong, as evidenced by its PassMark memory score.

From a resource density perspective, the gr6.8xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring both high memory capacity and GPU acceleration without necessitating top-tier multi-core CPU performance. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps networking supports data-heavy pipelines. This instance is highly suited for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference, image processing, and database operations that benefit from GPU acceleration and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services gr6.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, and 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside strong single-core CPU and memory performance. While multi-core CPU benchmarks remain average, the server's high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated GPU acceleration make it highly efficient for machine learning inference, image processing, and graphics-heavy workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services gr6.8xlarge is a memory-optimized, GPU-accelerated instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 900 GB NVMe SSD storage for demanding graphics and ML workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services gr6.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 900 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated, memory-optimized instance featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and local NVMe storage for high-throughput machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Features 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 1:8 resource ratio.
Equipped with a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM for graphics and ML acceleration.
Includes 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference workloads.
Suited for machine learning inference, image processing, and graphics-intensive applications.
